summary: P0 blockers #972 and #973 are CLOSED/COMPLETED — the hook deny reason retry metadata bug and essentials pack bundling issue are both resolved. However, all 8 open PRs are failing CI (test-and-build or lint), indicating a systemic baseline issue on main that needs a QA fix cycle before any PR can merge. PR budget is exceeded (8 open vs max 3), though most PRs are cross-squad. Sprint goal can now advance: with P0s cleared, #955 (Go kernel delegation) is unblocked once CI baseline is restored.

prs.title: fix(site): sync test count 2637→4161
prs.note: Site-only change; lint+test failing — likely merge base drift

prs.title: feat: OpenCode framework adapter (closes #1054)
prs.note: test-and-build passes, but lint and CodeQL failing. Lint fix needed.

prs.title: fix(format): prettier violations in storage and inspect (QA)
prs.note: Lint passes, test-and-build fails — merge base drift since main moved

prs.title: fix(adapters): educate mode always allows (closes #1026)
prs.note: Both lint and test-and-build failing. Needs rebase.

prs.title: feat(storage): parse driver:agent identity format (closes #1034)
prs.note: Lint clean, test-and-build fails — merge base drift

prs.title: feat(telemetry): add driverType field to AgentEvent (closes #1031)
test(storage): add coverage for agentId helpers and v5 migration (QA)
prs.note: Lint and test-and-build failing. QA report PR — stale candidate.

resolvedBlockers.title: Hook deny reason includes '(attempt N/N)' text — Claude Code blocks all tool use
claude-init --reset references essentials pack not bundled in npm

prBudget.note: 8 open PRs vs max 3 — budget exceeded. Most are cross-squad (adapters, telemetry, storage, site). Kernel squad should not open new PRs until backlog clears.

triage.note: No open P0 or P1 issues found with those labels. Both tracked P0s (#972, #973) are closed.
triage.sprintUpdate: P0s resolved — sprint can now progress to #955 (Go kernel delegation) and #957. However, CI must be restored first before any new PR can land.

recommendedNextActions.action: QA cycle: identify root cause of systemic test-and-build failure across all PRs on main
recommendedNextActions.note: All 8 PRs fail test-and-build. Likely a main-branch regression or a dependency change after recent merges. Fix CI baseline before pushing new work.

recommendedNextActions.action: Close stale QA report PRs (#1083, possibly #1108)
recommendedNextActions.note: QA report PRs accumulate and inflate PR budget. Close stale ones.

recommendedNextActions.action: Rebase and fix lint on #1104 (educate mode), #1087 (telemetry driverType), #1109 (OpenCode adapter)
recommendedNextActions.note: These close real issues and should land once CI baseline is restored.

recommendedNextActions.action: Resume #955 (Go kernel delegation) after CI is green
recommendedNextActions.note: P0 path is clear. This is the sprint goal.

blockers.description: Systemic test-and-build CI failure across all 8 open PRs
blockers.note: rust-kernel and CodeQL pass on most PRs — failure is in TS test-and-build job. Likely main-branch regression from recent merges. Needs QA investigation.

testHealth.note: Last local run was clean. CI test-and-build failures are on PR branches with older merge bases — main itself may still be clean.

escalationRules.notes: 2+ PRs failing CI → health stays YELLOW. P0 blockers resolved so no director escalation needed. PR budget exceeded but cross-squad context mitigates urgency.
